identity.variant: Basic / Standard / Customized (identical dimensions/weight; differ in DoF via end-effector and in compute platform)

No official discontinuation or successor statement exists for T1; T2 is an additional higher-tier flagship product sold in parallel, not a stated replacement for T1.

evidence.evidenceSummary: Product page and RoboCup solution page describe T1 as a bipedal walking robot (hip/knee/ankle joint ranges, walking/standing duration) common to all Basic/Standard/Customized configurations.
Page states dimensions 118×47×23cm and 'Approximately 1.2m'; explicitly identical across Basic/Standard/Customized ('identical core dimensions and weight').
Weight ~30kg stated as common across all three configurations.

Official page states walking duration ~2h and standing duration ~4h for the 10.5Ah battery, not differentiated by Basic/Standard/Customized. Record uses walking figure (120min) as the primary operating mode; standing (240min) noted separately.
Battery capacity (10.5Ah) and derived runtime figures stated as common across configurations; voltage not published so Wh cannot be computed.

identity.variant: Single known configuration (no Basic/Development split found, unlike K2)
identity.reason: K1's official product page is still live on gotokepler.com with an active ordering/contact channel, and remains referenced on the current homepage — it has not vanished or been marked discontinued. However the K1 page itself states the robot 'will be commercialized and mass-produced in near future', i.e. official material does not claim current mass-market shipping (unlike K2, which has a dated 2025-09-26 press release confirming mass production/customer shipping). This is treated as limited/developer-and-inquiry availability rather than full current production, and as distinct from an announced-only unreleased product since the page and ordering channel have existed since at least 2023-2024.
